content: Daemon lifecycle: On start, daemon connects to relay WebSocket with exponential backoff (base 1s, cap 60s, with jitter). Sends application-level ping every 30-45s (75% of typical proxy idle timeout). If N pings missed (typically 3), declares connection dead and reconnects. On reconnect, re-registers device credentials. Server tracks lastSeen timestamp per device connection to distinguish online/offline. Heartbeat acknowledgment timeout (typically 10s) before marking stale. Daemon runs as a background service (launchd on macOS, systemd on Linux) so it restarts on crash and survives machine reboots.
